description: Notes from the human editor for the decider to factor into triage and patching. Read at the start of every run. Mark notes as consumed after acting on them.

notes.author: operator (steve, via cowork session)
notes.subject: Drain mode: curmudgeon throttled to ≥4h. Burn down existing backlog without expecting fresh curmudgeon input.
notes.body:
OPERATOR ACTION

Operator is throttling the curmudgeon scheduled task from q30m → q240m (4h) — possibly slower. The curmudgeon→decider→analyst loop has been running fast enough to starve operator-priority work (Phase A1 design follow-ups, Phase B EXPs, EXP-211 revision). This HNOTE operationalizes the drain.

WHAT THIS MEANS FOR DECIDER

1. Stop re-flagging 'SCHEDULE MISMATCH' as recommended_action P1. The schedule will match BAU once the operator's UI change lands (curmudgeon q240m, analyst q120m, decider q240m). Until then it's transient — do not escalate.

2. Curmudgeon priority queue is currently depth 2 (WIN-025 qid 256, WIN-068 qid 257). Process those when they land via curmudgeon's slower cycle. Do NOT solicit fresh reviews. Do NOT auto-push more queue items unless an operator HNOTE explicitly requests it.

3. Drain priorities (in order):
   a. Apply any patches for already-existing curmudgeon-driven issues (open-issues.json) where suggested_patch is concrete and self-applicable.
   b. Integrate any analyst-delivered EXPs sitting in 'complete, integrated=false' state.
   c. Hand Mode 4 expansion-queue items to analyst as fast as possible. Once Mode 4 goes empty, analyst's Mode 2 (human notes) will fire on the Phase B HNOTEs.
   d. Standalone open issues without assigned_exp — process by severity, ignore the absence of curmudgeon flag.

4. ISS-1457, ISS-1458, ISS-1459 (Phase A1 design follow-ups) have been CLOSED BY OPERATOR at this commit. Patches landed directly in build-scripts/generate-html.js (infrastructure file, not in decider self-apply lane). Closures already moved from open-issues.json to closed-issues.json. Do not re-create these issues; do not re-flag in daily reports as outstanding.

5. ISS-1425 / EXP-211-redesign remains pending-human and waits for analyst Mode 2 redraft. Already directed via HNOTE-ISS-1425-OPERATOR-SIDES-WITH-CURMUDGEON-001. No decider action.

6. EXP-190 (Scoreboard) remains draft_awaiting_human_group_review. Operator deferred — no action.

EXPECTED EFFECT

Over the next 12-24 hours, curmudgeon's slower cadence will reduce new priority-queue items. Existing open-issues.json count (currently 104) should drift down as decider applies patches and analyst delivers EXPs. Once decider self-apply rate exceeds curmudgeon push rate, queue depth approaches zero and operator-priority HNOTEs (Phase B commission) get analyst attention.

CANCELLATION

This HNOTE expires when (a) operator restores curmudgeon to faster cadence with an explicit HNOTE, or (b) curmudgeon queue depth reaches 0 AND open-issues count < 50, whichever comes first. Decider can mark this consumed at that point.

STATUS: open.
